summary.intent: Add bundled default skills to Moltis by embedding skill files from Hermes Agent and OpenClaw repos directly in the binary, with category metadata, web UI browsing, and auto-install of missing CLI dependencies.
summary.outcome: Successfully shipped PR #797 with 100 bundled skills (from 2 upstream repos) embedded via include_dir!, category filtering/search UI, inline skill detail panel, system prompt integration, and install hint generation for missing binaries.

repo: Skills use SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter; discovery has 5 priority levels: Project > Personal > Registry > Plugin > Bundled — user-created skills always override bundled ones by name
Web assets and bundled skills both use include_dir! for compile-time embedding with dev-mode filesystem fallback — same three-tier pattern

The real system prompt entry point for skills is discover_skills_if_enabled() in crates/chat/src/prompt.rs, NOT the gateway services — missing this meant bundled skills never appeared in agent prompts
check-file-size.sh has an allowlist mechanism; .moltis/ and vendored assets directories need explicit exclusions
Biome is the TypeScript formatter (not Prettier); indentation inside JSX prop arrow functions must match nesting depth exactly — off-by-one-tab causes CI failures
The local-validate.sh script runs biome, tsc, fmt, i18n, zizmor, file-size, and install-docs checks in parallel before cargo builds
YAML unquoted @ character causes parse failures — scoped npm packages like @anthropic-ai/claude-code must be quoted in frontmatter
skill_tools.rs was refactored into a skill_tools/ module directory on main mid-PR, requiring conflict resolution and porting changes to read_ops.rs
Clippy in this repo uses -D clippy::expect-used in test code requires #[allow(clippy::expect_used)] in test modules, not substituting with unwrap_or_else

code.finding: map_or(true, String::is_empty) should be is_none_or(String::is_empty) per clippy::unnecessary_map_or
Test module needs #[allow(clippy::expect_used)] — clippy::expect-used is deny-level in this repo
(lo + hi + 1) / 2 must be written as (lo + hi).div_ceil(2) — clippy::manual_div_ceil is deny-level
discover_skills_if_enabled() is the system prompt skill injection point — was using plain FsSkillDiscoverer, not CompositeSkillDiscoverer, so bundled skills never appeared in agent context
api_skills_handler creates its own FsSkillDiscoverer — must be updated separately from post_state.rs to show bundled skills in web UI
run_install() runs on the gateway host process, not in the agent sandbox — install commands must be returned as hints for the agent to exec, not run server-side
Biome requires arrow function bodies inside JSX props to be indented one level deeper than the closing brace — multiple passes needed to get indentation right

workflow: Python one-liners are effective for bulk YAML frontmatter injection across 69+ files — sed is unreliable for multi-line YAML blocks and can truncate files
When porting skills from another repo, directory name must match SKILL.md frontmatter name exactly or read_skill() silently fails — 6 mismatches were caught by a discovery-vs-read test
Greptile PR review identified: bundled godmode (red-teaming) skill should not be enabled by default, path normalization missing on bundled sidecar reads, and intra-bundled deduplication gap
cargo check is sufficient for validating compilation across crates; full build with llama-cpp fills disk in worktrees
Background task notifications from Bash tool need explicit result reads — they don't auto-surface output

friction: skill_tools.rs was split into skill_tools/ module on main mid-PR, requiring conflict resolution and re-porting all bundled skill changes to read_ops.rs
npm auto-install ran on gateway host (wrong environment) — agent execs run in sandbox container which is a completely different environment; had to redesign to return install hints instead
@steipete/summarize EINVALIDPACKAGENAME — npm --ignore-scripts flag was initially added as a security measure but broke scoped package installs; removing it still failed due to host/sandbox mismatch
Biome indentation required 4+ fix attempts — arrow function bodies inside nested JSX props have context-dependent indentation rules that differ from standard JS formatting
sed corrupted SKILL.md files during bulk origin metadata injection (truncated files); required re-copying from source repo and switching to Python
The system prompt not showing bundled skills was a silent failure — agent appeared to work but had none of the bundled skills available for 2+ conversation turns before root cause was identified
Tailwind CSS style.css conflict on every main merge — generated file always conflicts, must be regenerated via `npx tailwindcss` after each merge
open_items: google-workspace skill is duplicated (one from Hermes using ~/.hermes OAuth, one from OpenClaw using gws CLI) — redundant bundled skills should be deduplicated
plan skill still references .hermes/plans/ directory in its body — missed in the Hermes cleanup pass
Ineligible skills (missing required binaries) show in system prompt without eligibility annotation — could add eligible="false" attribute to <skill> tags so agent knows before trying

Auto-install architecture is fundamentally broken for sandbox environments — a proper solution would need a signal from the agent's exec context or a pre-flight install endpoint scoped to the session sandbox
obliteratus skill (LLM refusal removal) is bundled by default — may warrant the same treatment as godmode (opt-in only) depending on policy decisions
Skills prompt compact fallback triggers at 30KB; with 100 skills at ~2.5KB total this is safe, but needs revisiting if skill count grows past ~400
